title: RNA Extraction Protocol for Aurantiochytrium limacinum
author: Michael Horowitz
license_note: Confirmed via CrossRef DOI metadata: dx.doi.org/10.17504/protocols.io.bffgjjjw
materials: NEB Monarch Total RNA Miniprep Kit
Use NEB Monarch Total RNA Miniprep Kit from here. All centrifugation steps should be carried out at 16,000rcf.

steps.title: Start a preculture 4 days prior...
steps.instruction: Start a preculture 4 days prior to extraction by inoculating 5 ml of 790 with a colony of Aurantiochytrium limacinum (ATCC MYA-1381). Incubate overnight at 28 C 171 rpm. Use preculture to inoculate 20 ml of 790 in a 250 ml flask. Culture for three days at 28 C, 171 rpm.

steps.title: Determine volume of culture needed to...
steps.instruction: Determine volume of culture needed to reach 5*10^7 cells via cell count.

steps.title: Place volume needed into a microcentrifuge...
steps.instruction: Place volume needed into a microcentrifuge tube and centrifuge down at 13000rpm for two minutes at room temperature. Discard supernatant.

steps.title: Place pellet in liquid nitrogen for 3-5 minutes
steps.instruction: Place pellet in liquid nitrogen for 3-5 minutes.

steps.title: Resuspend pellet in 750ul Trizol lysis...
steps.instruction: Resuspend pellet in 750ul Trizol lysis buffer and incubate on ice for 1 hour and 30 minutes.

steps.title: Add 150ul chloroform in fume hood...
steps.instruction: Add 150ul chloroform in fume hood to the pellet and let sit for 2 minutes.

steps.title: Vigorously shake the mixture and centrifuge...
steps.instruction: Vigorously shake the mixture and centrifuge at 13000rpm for 5 minutes at 4C.

steps.title: Pipette upper aqueous layer to a...
steps.instruction: Pipette upper aqueous layer to a sterile microcentrifuge tube on ice.

steps.title: Add an equal volume of >95%...
steps.instruction: Add an equal volume of >95% ethanol to the upper aqueous layer and mix well.

steps.title: Take an RNA purification column and collection tube
steps.instruction: Take an RNA purification column and collection tube. Place the mixture (the upper aqueous layer with ethanol) from the microcentrifuge tube to the purification column.

steps.title: Centrifuge for 30 seconds and discard flow-through
steps.instruction: Centrifuge for 30 seconds and discard flow-through.

steps.title: Add 500ul RNA Wash Buffer and spin for 30 seconds
steps.instruction: Add 500ul RNA Wash Buffer and spin for 30 seconds. Discard flow-through.

steps.title: In a separate RNase-free microcentrifuge tube,...
steps.instruction: In a separate RNase-free microcentrifuge tube, combine 5ul DNase 1 provided in the Monarch Kit with 75ul DNase 1 Reaction Buffer. Pipette the mixture directly onto the column matrix.

steps.title: Incubate for 15 minutes at room temperature
steps.instruction: Incubate for 15 minutes at room temperature.

steps.title: Add 500ul RNA Priming Buffer and spin for 30 seconds
steps.instruction: Add 500ul RNA Priming Buffer and spin for 30 seconds. Discard the flow-through.

steps.title: Add 500ul RNA Wash Buffer and spin for 30 seconds
steps.instruction: Add 500ul RNA Wash Buffer and spin for 30 seconds. Discard the flow-through.

steps.title: Add another 500ul RNA Wash Buffer...
steps.instruction: Add another 500ul RNA Wash Buffer and spin for two minutes . Transfer the column to a new RNase-free microcentrifuge tube.

steps.title: Add 30ul of Nuclease-free Water directly...
steps.instruction: Add 30ul of Nuclease-free Water directly to the column matrix and spin for 30 seconds.

steps.title: Pipette the flow-through back into the...
steps.instruction: Pipette the flow-through back into the column matrix and spin again for 30 seconds.

steps.title: Remove column matrix, close the microcentrifuge tube, and label it
steps.instruction: Remove column matrix, close the microcentrifuge tube, and label it. Store the eluted RNA at -20C for short-term storage or at -80C for long-term storage. RNA yields for GPY grown cells are usually around a 1000ng/ul and for 790-grown cells are much lower around 50-100ng/ul. Lower yield for 790 cells are possibly due to excessive cell clumping.
